hypothesis: For three- or four-component Ar/O2/other-gas mixtures in the same ICP reactor, augmenting the inductive GraphSAGE model with edge features computed as pairwise chemical-interaction scores (e.g., Jaccard similarity of reaction sets or normalized differences in key ionization/adsorption rates derived from single-gas PBMs) will improve mixture etching-rate prediction R2 by at least 0.05 versus the identical GraphSAGE architecture using an unweighted complete graph when trained and evaluated on the same mixture PBM dataset.
